mechanism_narrative: A sustained quarterly growth in GDP (GDPC1) signals strong economic demand, which drives corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods of GDPC1 growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 320 indicates elevated inflation, prompting expectations of tighter monetary policy and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 320 despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL signal a tightening cycle with stagflation risks, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint movement of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fails to predict SPX returns, with Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising FEDFUNDS compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns during FEDFUNDS increases are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions, driving consumer spending and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns in PAYEMS growth periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in INDPRO signals expanding industrial activity, boosting corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day SPX returns in INDPRO growth periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in RSAFS (consumer spending) and HOUST (housing starts) indicates strong demand-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint movement of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict SPX returns, with Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly UNRATE below 4% signals a tight labor market, increasing wage pressures and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE falls below 4% despite the signal.
